title: sphaeralcea rusbyi subsp. eremicola
intro: Sphaeralcea rusbyi subsp. eremicola, commonly known as Rusby's globemallow, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mallow family (Malvaceae). It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Rusby's globemallow can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support overall health and well-being, as well as to treat specific ailments.
